action.reasoning: The task requires producing a 4-chapter technical field guide with research, drafting, editing, a glossary, and assembly. This is too large for a single EXECUTE action. I will decompose it into parallel chapter subtasks (each handling research, draft, and edit for one chapter), a glossary subtask, and take the final assembly role myself. Each chapter subtask will produce its own chapter content. The glossary will be built in parallel. I will then assemble everything into the final guide.

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 1: Introduction to LLM Agent Memory Systems

subtasks.goal: Research vector database architectures for LLM long-term memory
subtasks.capsule: Investigate vector databases (e.g., FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus) used for storing and retrieving semantic embeddings in agent memory systems. Cover indexing strategies (HNSW, IVF), similarity metrics, scalability, and trade-offs. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on vector database architectures for agent memory

subtasks.goal: Research knowledge graph approaches for agent memory
subtasks.capsule: Investigate knowledge graph-based memory systems, including graph databases (Neo4j, Amazon Neptune), RDF/OWL, entity-relationship modeling, and graph neural networks for reasoning. Cover how agents store structured facts, relationships, and perform graph traversal for retrieval.

outputs.description: Research summary on knowledge graph approaches for agent memory

subtasks.goal: Research episodic memory and RAG patterns for agents
subtasks.capsule: Investigate episodic memory stores (experience replay, trajectory logging) and retrieval-augmented generation patterns (dense retrieval, hybrid search, re-ranking, memory consolidation). Cover how agents recall past interactions and integrate retrieved context into generation.

outputs.description: Research summary on episodic memory and RAG patterns for agents

action.reasoning: Chapter 4 is a substantial technical writing task covering multiple advanced topics (consolidation, multi-agent sharing, debugging, future directions). It requires original research, drafting, and editing. To ensure depth and quality, I will decompose it into parallel research subtasks for each major topic, then integrate them into a cohesive draft and perform a final edit. This approach allows parallel work on independent topics while maintaining a clear integration path.

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on memory consolidation techniques
subtasks.capsule: Investigate and write about memory consolidation patterns in LLM agents, including rehearsal strategies, distillation methods, hierarchical summarization, and decay mechanisms. This section should cover how agents transition short-term working memory into long-term stored knowledge, with concrete examples and architectural patterns.

outputs.description: Research notes and draft text on memory consolidation techniques for LLM agents

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on multi-agent memory sharing
subtasks.capsule: Investigate and write about how multiple LLM agents share, synchronize, and coordinate through shared memory systems. Cover topics like shared memory spaces, memory routing, conflict resolution, privacy boundaries, and emergent coordination patterns. Include architectural diagrams described in text.

outputs.description: Research notes and draft text on multi-agent memory sharing patterns

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on memory debugging and observability
subtasks.capsule: Investigate and write about techniques for debugging, monitoring, and observing LLM agent memory systems. Cover logging strategies, memory state inspection, tracing memory access patterns, identifying memory leaks or corruption, and tools/frameworks for memory observability.

outputs.description: Research notes and draft text on memory debugging and observability techniques

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on emerging research directions
subtasks.capsule: Investigate and write about cutting-edge research directions in LLM agent memory, including neuro-symbolic memory, embodied memory, continuous learning without catastrophic forgetting, memory-augmented architectures beyond standard RAG, and open research questions. This section should be forward-looking and identify key challenges.

outputs.description: Research notes and draft text on emerging research directions in agent memory

self_role.goal: Integrate all section drafts into a cohesive Chapter 4 and perform final consistency edit

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 4 on advanced memory patterns and future directions
